Blockframes

Introduction

A single SVG supplies the elements that make up the blockframe library.

Decoration

The Painter class styles blocks by applying the numeric values for colors in the current Tailwind theme. This is done to ensure the colors are available at runtime without hard-coding before build.

Animation

The Animator class handles (you guessed it) animations. It can be tricky to manage animations of individual elements within an SVG given how elements get rendered based on viewport size and scale. In the example below, if the distance traveled by the block was greater than the SVG's viewport, then the block would begin its motion offscreen. Instead, the distance traveled by the block is determined by the size of its containing SVG. See Animator.wipe for details.

The approach I use could definitely use some refinement.For example, the origin for the motion does not change with screensize.Also, in terms of the bigger picture(no pun intended)I would love to have the ability to set an element 's dimensions in pixels and have its size remain constant even when the dimensions of the SVG change.

Animating Color

Create a grid of blocks. Animate their color using the GSAP grid methods. Connect it to ScrollTrigger.
